title: Snip, Fold, and Unfold Winter Magic!
story: Grab your scissors because December 27 is Make Cut-Out Snowflakes Day! People everywhere celebrate by folding white paper into triangles, then carefully snipping away tiny pieces to create beautiful snowflake patterns. When you unfold the paper, surprise! You've made a unique snowflake that's completely different from anyone else's. Families hang their paper creations in windows, tape them to walls, or string them together as decorations. Some people make giant snowflakes, while others create tiny delicate ones. The best part? No two snowflakes ever turn out exactly the same, just like real snow!
activity_question: What happens when you unfold your cut paper on Make Cut-Out Snowflakes Day?
activity_choices: You discover a unique snowflake pattern
The paper turns into real snow
activity_answer: You discover a unique snowflake pattern
follow_up_question: What creative patterns would you cut to make your snowflake extra special?

title: Wild Fun at the Zoo Day!
story: December 27 is Visit the Zoo Day, when families everywhere bundle up and head to their local zoos for winter adventures! Even though it's chilly, many animals are actually more active in cooler weather. Polar bears splash in icy pools, penguins waddle around happily, and big cats prowl with extra energy. Zoos often offer special holiday activities like animal keeper talks, hot cocoa stations, and festive decorations throughout the exhibits. Some zoos even have indoor tropical houses where you can warm up while watching colorful birds and reptiles. It's the perfect way to get outside and learn something new after the holidays!
activity_question: Which animals are mentioned as being more active in cooler weather?
follow_up_question: What's the most interesting animal behavior you've noticed during a winter zoo visit?

title: Picture Perfect Rescue Day
story: Every December 27, families across the country dig through dusty boxes and old phones to save their precious photos! National Save Your Photos Day reminds people to organize, back up, and protect their favorite memories before they fade or get lost. Kids help parents scan old pictures, create digital albums, and share funny baby photos with relatives. Many families use this day to make photo books or upload thousands of pictures to cloud storage so they'll never disappear.
activity_question: What do families often do with old photos on National Save Your Photos Day?
follow_up_question: What's the oldest photo in your family's collection?

title: Shh! It's No Interruptions Day
story: December 27 is National No Interruptions Day, when people try their hardest to let others finish talking without butting in! Families practice taking turns during conversations at dinner. Teachers remind students to raise their hands and wait. Some offices even put up "listening ears" signs to help everyone remember. It's trickier than you think to bite your tongue when you have something exciting to share, but giving others space to speak makes everyone feel heard and respected.
activity_question: What do some offices put up on No Interruptions Day to help people remember to listen?
follow_up_question: What's the hardest situation for you to avoid interrupting someone?

title: Picture Perfect Day for Better Images
story: December 27 is Build a Better Image Day, when people focus on improving how they present themselves to the world! Some folks update their social media profiles with fresh photos, while others practice better posture or work on their confidence. It's not about being fake – it's about putting your best foot forward and feeling good about yourself.
activity_question: What is one way people celebrate Build a Better Image Day?
follow_up_question: What's one small thing you could do to build a better image of yourself?
